when_to_use: Requested user-visible symbols were returned with expected data fields.
user_visible_evidence: Returned symbols match requested symbols; no visible error.
not_evidence_of: Does not prove all fields are complete or current.
recommended_next_test: Run representative batch and compare key fields.

when_to_use: Some requested symbols returned and some did not.
user_visible_evidence: Returned symbol count is lower than requested count.
not_evidence_of: Not automatically a Yahoo outage or invalid symbol.
recommended_next_test: Retest missing symbols one at a time.

when_to_use: A requested symbol is absent from the app export/result list.
user_visible_evidence: AAPL requested but result begins with MSFT or omits AAPL.
not_evidence_of: Does not prove AAPL is invalid.
recommended_next_test: Run AAPL alone, then AAPL+MSFT, then full batch.

when_to_use: App returns an empty result list for a supported symbol input.
user_visible_evidence: No rows returned; no raw JSON visible.
not_evidence_of: Not enough to identify Yahoo vs app parser cause.
recommended_next_test: Retest with MSFT as control; check if any error text is visible.
project_action: Classify with low/medium confidence unless raw status/body visible.

when_to_use: App displays null or no object where data was expected.
user_visible_evidence: Null body, blank output, or quoteSummary result null.
recommended_next_test: Record exact visible text and whether fundamentals lookup was involved.
project_action: Mark as ambiguous without raw endpoint response.

when_to_use: A full Yahoo URL appears inside a quote/fundamentals 'symbol not found' message.
user_visible_evidence: Error says quote not found for symbol: HTTPS://QUERY1...
not_evidence_of: Not evidence the Search/Chart/Screener endpoint failed.
recommended_next_test: Do not use URL inputs in symbol lookup unless app docs say URLs are supported.

when_to_use: The app symbol lookup field appears unable to process raw URLs.
user_visible_evidence: URL input yields invalid symbol behavior or empty result.
not_evidence_of: Not evidence the Yahoo endpoint URL is invalid.
recommended_next_test: Ask vendor whether direct URL mode exists; otherwise test symbols only.
project_action: Move URL endpoints to documentation-only or direct-browser tests.

when_to_use: Observed behavior indicates the feature is for ticker symbols or symbol lists.
user_visible_evidence: Normal symbols work; full URLs are treated as symbols.
not_evidence_of: Does not limit Yahoo API capabilities.
recommended_next_test: Use only symbol and symbol-list tests in the app.
project_action: Frame all app results as black-box symbol lookup results.

when_to_use: The app hides raw HTTP status, URL, or JSON response.
not_evidence_of: Cannot prove exact API request or parser internals.
recommended_next_test: Capture app version, input, output, and export file.
project_action: Use lower confidence unless independently verified.

when_to_use: Direct HTTP testing shows Too Many Requests.
user_visible_evidence: HTTP status 429 visible from direct test.
not_evidence_of: Not visible unless app exposes status or direct test is run.
recommended_next_test: Retry later with lower frequency and user-agent/browser context.

when_to_use: An endpoint appears to be parsed by the wrong routine.
user_visible_evidence: Search/Chart URL is reported by quote/fundamentals lookup.
not_evidence_of: Not necessarily modifiable by the user.
recommended_next_test: Treat as app behavior unless source code access exists.

when_to_use: Fields exist but shape/path/type differs from earlier capture.
user_visible_evidence: Raw response visible and differs from documented schema.
not_evidence_of: Not the same as empty or null result.
recommended_next_test: Capture raw JSON and compare field paths.
project_action: Update master field database only with evidence.

when_to_use: Symbols with =, ^, or - are missing from a batch while simple symbols return.
not_evidence_of: Not proof the symbols are invalid; may be encoding/app limitation.
recommended_next_test: Run raw and URL-encoded variants if app allows; otherwise test singly.

when_to_use: A Yahoo endpoint cannot be meaningfully tested through the symbol lookup box.
user_visible_evidence: Search/Screener/Chart URL entered into symbol field.
recommended_next_test: Use browser/direct HTTP outside the app or omit endpoint test.
project_action: Keep project documentation clear about access limits.
